info.title: Bootstrap Agent Lockstep API
info.description: Lockstep API for rack initialization and reset

get.summary: Get the current contents of the network config kept in the replicated
get.description:
bootstore.

This should ONLY be used for debugging (e.g., via `omdb`). Nexus, RSS, and sled-agent should never access this endpoint in production - the bootstore contents should be treated as "write only" from their point of view.

get.summary: Get the status of the "scrimlet reconcilers" subsystem.
get.description: This should ONLY be used for debugging (e.g., via `omdb`). This is akin to the "background task status" exposed by the Nexus lockstep API. If we find a need for any of the information contained in this endpoint for production, we should refine this, version it, and (probably?) move it to inventory.

get.summary: Get the current status of rack initialization or reset.

post.summary: Initialize the rack with the provided configuration.

delete.summary: Reset the rack to an unconfigured state.

AllowedSourceIps.description: Description of source IPs allowed to reach rack services.

oneOf.description: Allow traffic from any external IP address.

Restrict access to a specific set of source IP addresses or subnets.

All others are prevented from reaching rack services.

Baseboard.description: Describes properties that should uniquely identify a Gimlet.

asn.description: The autonomous system number for the BGP configuration.

checker.description: Checker to apply to incoming messages.

max_paths.description: Maximum number of paths to use when multiple "best paths" exist

originate.description: The set of prefixes for the BGP router to originate.

shaper.description: Shaper to apply to outgoing messages.

allowed_export.description: Define export policy for a peer.

allowed_import.description: Define import policy for a peer.

asn.description: The autonomous system number of the router the peer belongs to.

communities.description: Include the provided communities in updates sent to the peer.

connect_retry.description: The interval in seconds between peer connection retry attempts.

delay_open.description: How long to delay sending open messages to a peer. In seconds.

enforce_first_as.description: Enforce that the first AS in paths received from this peer is the peer's AS.

hold_time.description: How long to keep a session alive without a keepalive in seconds. Defaults to 6.

idle_hold_time.description: How long to keep a peer in idle after a state machine reset in seconds.

keepalive.description: The interval to send keepalive messages at.

local_pref.description: Apply a local preference to routes received from this peer.

md5_auth_key.description: Use the given key for TCP-MD5 authentication with the peer.

min_ttl.description: Require messages from a peer have a minimum IP time to live field.

multi_exit_discriminator.description: Apply the provided multi-exit discriminator (MED) updates sent to the peer.

port.description: Switch port the peer is reachable on.

remote_asn.description: Require that a peer has a specified ASN.

vlan_id.description: Associate a VLAN ID with a BGP peer session.

oneOf.description: Ignore all bootstrap addresses except our own.
Ignore all bootstrap addresses except the following.

DetermineSwitchSlotStatus.description: Status of attempting to determine this sled's switch slot via MGS within this sled's switch zone.

oneOf.description: We're not attempting to contact MGS because we're not a scrimlet.

We're currently attempting to contact MGS.

If this is not the first attempt, `prev_attempt_err` contains the error we encountered the last time. (If the last time succeeded, we'd be done!)
We're currently idle waiting for a timeout to retry due to a previous failure.

DpdNatReconcilerStatus.description: Status of reconciling service zone NAT entries with `dpd`.

oneOf.description: Reconciliation was skipped because the bootstore contains no NAT entry config information.
Reconciliation failed while attempting to read the current set of entries from `dpd`.
Reconciliation failed because the bootstore config contained an illegal combination of entries (e.g., two zones with identical NAT entries).
Reconciliation completed successfully.

created.description: Map of zone NAT entries created.

unchanged.description: Set of zone IDs whose NAT entries were already correct in `dpd` and left unchanged.

oneOf.description: Reconciliation completed but had at least one failure.

create_failures.description: Map of zone NAT entries we tried but failed to create.

created.description: Map of zone NAT entries successfully created.

remove_failures.description: List of NAT entries we tried but failed to remove.

removed.description: List of NAT entries successfully removed.

unchanged.description: Set of zone IDs whose NAT entries were already correct in `dpd` and left unchanged.

DpdPortReconcilerStatus.description: Status of reconciling QSFP port settings with `dpd`.

oneOf.description: Reconciliation failed while attempting to read the current settings from `dpd`.
Reconciliation failed because the data prevented us from constructing a plan - this should be impossible absent bugs.
Reconciliation completed successfully.
Reconciliation completed but had at least one failure.

nat_status.description: Result of reconciling service zone NAT entries

ImportExportPolicy.description: Define policy relating to the import and export of prefixes from a BGP peer.

oneOf.description: Do not perform any filtering.

Ipv4Net.title: An IPv4 subnet
Ipv4Net.description: An IPv4 subnet, including prefix and prefix length

Ipv4Range.description:
A non-decreasing IPv4 address range, inclusive of both ends.

The first address must be less than or equal to the last address.

Ipv6Net.title: An IPv6 subnet
Ipv6Net.description: An IPv6 subnet, including prefix and subnet mask

Ipv6Range.description:
A non-decreasing IPv6 address range, inclusive of both ends.

The first address must be less than or equal to the last address.

LinkFec.description: The forward error correction mode of a link.

oneOf.description: Firecode forward error correction.
No forward error correction.
Reed-Solomon forward error correction.
Zero gigabits per second.
1 gigabit per second.
10 gigabits per second.
25 gigabits per second.
40 gigabits per second.
50 gigabits per second.
100 gigabits per second.
200 gigabits per second.
400 gigabits per second.

LldpAdminStatus.description: To what extent should this port participate in LLDP

LldpPortConfig.description: Per-port LLDP configuration settings.  Only the "status" setting is mandatory.  All other fields have natural defaults or may be inherited from the switch.

chassis_id.description: Chassis ID to advertise.  If this is set, it will be advertised as a LocallyAssigned ID type.  If this is not set, it will be inherited from the switch-level settings.

management_addrs.description: Management IP addresses to advertise.  If this is not set, it will be inherited from the switch-level settings.

port_description.description: Port description to advertise.  If this is not set, no description will be advertised.

port_id.description: Port ID to advertise.  If this is set, it will be advertised as a LocallyAssigned ID type.  If this is not set, it will be set to the port name. e.g., qsfp0/0.

status.description: To what extent should this port participate in LLDP

system_description.description: System description to advertise.  If this is not set, it will be inherited from the switch-level settings.

system_name.description: System name to advertise.  If this is not set, it will be inherited from the switch-level settings.

oneOf.description: Reconciliation was skipped because we couldn't fetch the current set of BFD peers from mgd.
Reconciliation completed successfully.
Reconciliation completed.
Reconciliation was skipped because we couldn't fetch the current BGP configuration from MGD.

Reconciliation was skipped because we couldn't generate a desired config from the bootstore.

This should never happen - it indicates there's faulty data in the persisted config.

Reconciliation completed successfully.

mgd operations are performed in bulk, so each item here contains the count of items involved.

Reconciliation completed with at least one failure.

mgd operations are performed in bulk, so each item here contains the count of items applied on success.
Reconciliation was skipped because we couldn't fetch the current set of static routes from MGD.

Reconciliation was skipped because we couldn't determine a plan for changes to make.

This should never happen - it indicates there's some faulty data somewhere (either coming from mgd or in the rack network config).

Reconciliation completed successfully.

mgd operations are performed in bulk, so each item here contains the count of items involved.

Reconciliation completed with at least one failure.

mgd operations are performed in bulk, so each item here contains the count of items applied on success.

Name.title: A name unique within the parent collection
Name.description: Names must begin with a lower case ASCII letter, be composed exclusively of lowercase ASCII, uppercase ASCII, numbers, and '-', and may not end with a '-'. Names cannot be a UUID, but they may contain a UUID. They can be at most 63 characters long.

NewPasswordHash.title: A password hash in PHC string format
NewPasswordHash.description: Password hashes must be in PHC (Password Hashing Competition) string format.  Passwords must be hashed with Argon2id.  Password hashes may be rejected if the parameters appear not to be secure enough.

addresses.description: This port's addresses and optional vlan IDs

autoneg.description: Whether or not to set autonegotiation

port.description: Name of the port this config applies to.

routes.description: The set of routes associated with this port.

RackInitializeRequest.description:
Configuration for the "rack setup service".

The Rack Setup Service should be responsible for one-time setup actions, such as CockroachDB placement and initialization.  Without operator intervention, however, these actions need a way to be automated in our deployment.

allowed_source_ips.description: IPs or subnets allowed to make requests to user-facing services

bootstrap_discovery.description: Describes how bootstrap addresses should be collected during RSS.

external_certificates.description: initial TLS certificates for the external API

external_dns_ips.description:
Service IP addresses on which we run external DNS servers.

Each address must be present in `internal_services_ip_pool_ranges`.

external_dns_zone_name.description: DNS name for the DNS zone delegated to the rack for external DNS

external_jumbo_frames_opt_in_enabled.description: When true, end users may opt in to jumbo frames on the primary interface of an instance, and control plane services with external-facing OPTE NICs are brought up with an 8500 byte MTU. Operators can toggle this at runtime via the fleet networking settings API.

internal_services_ip_pool_ranges.description: Ranges of the service IP pool which may be used for internal services.

recovery_silo.description: Configuration of the Recovery Silo (the initial Silo)

trust_quorum_peers.description:
The set of peer_ids required to initialize trust quorum

The value is `None` if we are not using trust quorum

bfd.description: BFD configuration for connecting the rack to external networks

bgp.description: BGP configurations for connecting the rack to external networks

infra_ip_first.description: First ip address to be used for configuring network infrastructure

infra_ip_last.description: Last ip address to be used for configuring network infrastructure

ports.description: Uplinks for connecting the rack to external networks

RackOperationStatus.description: Current status of any rack-level operation being performed by this bootstrap agent.

oneOf.description: `id` will be none if the rack was already initialized on startup.
`reset_id` will be None if the rack is in an uninitialized-on-startup, or Some if it is in an uninitialized state due to a reset operation completing.

ReconcilerActivationReason.description: Why a reconciler task was activated.

oneOf.description: Each reconciler runs once on startup.
The task was activated due to its periodic timer firing.
The task was activated in response to a change in the networking config.
The task was activated in response to the sled becoming a scrimlet again (after previously transitioning to "not a scrimlet").
The reconciler is inert: it will not or cannot run for some reason.
The reconciler is currently running.
The reconciler is not currently running.

ReconcilerInertReason.description: Why a reconciler task has gone inert.

oneOf.description: The reconciler task started when this sled was a scrimlet, but it has since become "not a scrimlet" (e.g., because the attached switch has gone away).
The reconciler task exited. This is not expected except in tests; the task runs forever as long as sled-agent holds on to the channels used to communicate with it.

current_status.description: Status of the task at this moment.

last_completion.description: Final status of the most recent activation of this task.

current_status.description: Status of the task at this moment.

last_completion.description: Final status of the most recent activation of this task.

current_status.description: Status of the task at this moment.

last_completion.description: Final status of the most recent activation of this task.

current_status.description: Status of the task at this moment.

last_completion.description: Final status of the most recent activation of this task.

RecoverySiloConfig.description: Configuration for the recovery silo created during rack setup.

ReplicatedNetworkConfig.description: Wrapper for optional contents of the replicated network config.

ReplicatedNetworkConfigContents.description:
Contents of the replicated network config.

Analogous to the bootstore `NetworkConfig` type.

base64_blob.description:
The `NetworkConfig` arbitrary blob, encoded using the standard base64 alphabet.

Currently, the contents are expected to be JSON, but serialization/deserialization of the contents is performed outside the replication engine, which just deals with a binary blob.

rib_priority.description: The RIB priority (i.e. Admin Distance) associated with this route.

vlan_id.description: The VLAN id associated with this route.

RouterLifetimeConfig.description: Router lifetime in seconds for unnumbered BGP peers

router_lifetime.description: Router lifetime in seconds for unnumbered BGP peers.

ip.description: IP address for numbered BGP peers.

RssStep.description: Steps we go through during initial rack setup. Keep this list in order that they happen.

oneOf.description: `sled-agent` has not yet provided underlay networking information.
We're attempting to determine our switch slot.
We are a scrimlet and the individual reconcilers are running.
Switch in upper slot
Switch in lower slot

TxEqConfig.description: Per-port tx-eq overrides.  This can be used to fine-tune the transceiver equalization settings to improve signal integrity.

address.description: The address to be used on the uplink.

vlan_id.description: The VLAN id (if any) associated with this address.

UserId.title: A username for a local-only user
UserId.description: Usernames must begin with a lower case ASCII letter, be composed exclusively of lowercase ASCII, uppercase ASCII, numbers, and '-', and may not end with a '-'. Usernames cannot be a UUID, but they may contain a UUID. They can be at most 63 characters long.
